pipeline_status.poller: QUIET — 18 consecutive quiet polls. Dome site V51.0 unchanged. April Reset Block expires tomorrow (Apr 9). Pipeline cadence recovered to normal.
pipeline_status.analyst: Mode 2 (EXP-035 complete: WIN-052 MOND relabeling). Mode 3 (WIN-064 globe fingerprint: delta=6,371km=R_Earth, angular coords are spherical notation, shadow boundaries encode r_core/R_Earth ratio). 64/99 fingerprints done.
pipeline_status.curmudgeon: Cycle 2, Phase 1: 61/67 WINs reviewed. Last: WIN-061 (Schumann G3 storm). WIN-060 and WIN-061 C2 reviews have 0 holes (minor recommendations only). Remaining: WIN-062-067.

external_changes.dome_site_changes: No changes. All 7 pages checked, no substantive updates detected.

mode: MODE 1 (patchable critical/major existed at run start — ISS-633 WIN-054 major), then MODE 2 cleanup after EXP-035 integration

internal_issues.description: EXP-035: MOND priority underrepresented, galaxy ontology underplayed, MOND vs ΛCDM framing confusion
internal_issues.action_taken: Integrated EXP-035: (1) detail_claim updated to specify 0.057 dex scatter (removing 'near-zero' claim), (2) dome's ρ_A = g₀ρ_bar/g† formula explicitly identified as MOND's interpolation relation (g† = MOND's a₀ ≈ 1.2×10⁻¹⁰ m/s²) — dome lists it as CONSISTENT not CONFIRMED, all symbols undefined, (3) simplicity counter-argument added, (4) detail_verdict_text adds MOND relabeling point

internal_issues.description: Finding conflated two incompatible framings: genuine ΛCDM anomaly vs cherry-picking
internal_issues.action_taken: Rewrote finding to commit clearly: El Gordo is genuine 6.2σ tension, dome correctly identifies it, but dome accommodation Not Demonstrated

internal_issues.description: Kim et al. 2021 (ApJ 923, 101) citation accuracy: web search confirms paper exists and is about El Gordo at z=0.87 with new HST wide-field imaging (DOI valid). Paper reports NW subcluster 9.9×10¹⁴ M_☉ + SE subcluster 6.5×10¹⁴ M_☉ = ~1.64×10¹⁵ total. Our text cites total M₂₀₀c ≈ 2.13×10¹⁵ — this total may come from a different analysis (possibly Asencio et al. 2023's mass estimate or pre-2021 total). Requires manual verification of whether Kim et al. 2021 quotes a total cluster mass of 2.13×10¹⁵ M_☉.
internal_issues.action_taken: DEFERRED: citation DOI verified valid, paper covers correct subject. Cannot confirm specific 2.13×10¹⁵ total mass figure without full paper access. Human should verify and update if needed.
Added explicit derivation: best-case 6.2% RMSE vs sub-0.01% = ~620×; worst-case 7.3% NH error = ~730×

internal_issues.description: WIN-004/005/060 share dataset but redundancy unexplained
internal_issues.action_taken: Added: 'Reading independent properties (separation, decay rate, drift direction) from the same INTERMAGNET/Swarm dataset without independent dome-geometric derivation for each does not multiply predictive power — it is one observation parsed three ways.'

internal_issues.description: Cross-references claimed absent but already present
internal_issues.action_taken: Closed as already-fixed: WIN-053 already references 'Section 6.8: Globe Values Relabeled' and WIN-055 already references WIN-047 and Section 9.1 Pattern #4

internal_issues.description: WIN-061 citation imprecise — general Nickolaenko reference for storm-specific suppression
internal_issues.action_taken: Added Schlegel & Füllekrug 1999 (JGR 104, 10111) as primary storm-specific citation supplementing general reference

internal_issues.description: WIN-061 Live Power Dashboard 'Unscored' argument untapped
internal_issues.action_taken: Added explicit reference: dome's own Live Power Dashboard scores WIN-061 as 'Unscored — no G3+ storm observed since deployment' — confirming by dome's own metrics that this test has never run

internal_issues.description: Misleading verdict category overload — conflates 4 distinct failure modes
internal_issues.action_taken: DEFERRED to human: requires structural verdict taxonomy decision. Verdict changes are gated for human review.

internal_issues.description: Exhibit A and B coverage gap and statistical claims not addressed
internal_issues.action_taken: EXP-036 already queued as next analyst priority. Decider cannot add Exhibit A/B section without substantial research into dome source code and 2003 storm data.

internal_issues.description: Duplicate id='dielectric' in published HTML (part1 Section 1.6) and commented-out HTML (part4 Section 4.5)
internal_issues.action_taken: Renamed commented-out id to 'dielectric-infographic' in sections.json part4

recommended_actions.action: HUMAN: Verify WIN-054 total cluster mass citation (ISS-634). Kim et al. 2021 reports individual subclusters (~1.64×10¹⁵ total), not the 2.13×10¹⁵ M_☉ our text cites. Check Asencio et al. 2023 for their cited total mass figure.
HUMAN: Resolve ISS-442 (Misleading verdict taxonomy). Decide whether to split the category or add subcategory labels. This is a structural review decision.
ANALYST: EXP-036 (Exhibit A and B coverage gap) — should be next analyst run priority. Covers dome's home page 2003 Bentheimer storm correlation claims.
CURMUDGEON: 6 WINs remain in Cycle 2 (WIN-062 through WIN-067). Tesla longitudinal wave, magnetic decay asymmetry, P-wave shadow zone, Polaris excess, NH heat asymmetry, Antarctic gravity hole.

notes: Very productive run. EXP-035 fully integrated. 11 patches self-applied across 2 commits. Open issue count dropped from 13 to 4. Only ISS-634 (citation verification) and ISS-442 (verdict taxonomy — human decision) remain as non-analyst open items.
